summary: This proposal introduces a design for "minimum value selection" storage proofs on Merkle trees. The design consists of two main components: 1. A hashing algorithm termed MixHash, aimed to replace the commonly used Keccak256 and SHA256 algorithms. 2. Public data storage proofs. This enables anyone to present a proof to a public network, verifying their possession of a copy of specific public data marked by MixHash. Additionally, the proposal discusses the practical implementation of this design in various scenarios and suggests some improvements to the [ERC-721](./eip-721.md) and [ERC-1155](./eip-1155.md) standards.
title: MixHash and Public Data Storage Proofs
